name: Sierra de Aitana Loop Trail
name_original: La Serra d'Aitana — Trail run circular desde la Font del Partagat
description: Tackle the difficult 4.4-mile La Serra d'Aitana circular trail run from la Font del Partagat, gaining 1622 feet for panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Benifato offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by the rugged Sierra de Aitana mountain range. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often traversing mountainous terrain and offering views of natural springs and reservoirs. Many trails explore the areas around Font de Forata and Simas de Partagat, providing a challenging yet rewarding experience for runners. The varied topography ensures a dynamic environment for running. Best jogging routes around Benifato The most popular jogging route is Sierra de Aitana Loop Trail, a difficult 4.4 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This route features substantial climbs within the Sierra de Aitana. Another top favourite among local runners is Font de Forata – Simas de Partagat loop from Benifato, a difficult 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path. This trail explores natural springs and unique rock formations. Local runners also love the Font de Forata – Simas de Partagat loop from Serra d'Aitana, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and natural features, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around Benifato is defined by mountainous terrain, natural springs, and reservoir views. The network offers options primarily for moderate to advanced ability levels. The routes in Benifato are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 1600 runners have used komoot to explore Benifato's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Benifato?
A: Benifato's terrain is predominantly mountainous, making most running routes quite challenging. While there are no routes classified as 'easy', you can find 10 routes of moderate difficulty. The majority, 56 routes, are considered difficult, often involving significant elevation changes within the Sierra de Aitana.

Q: What are the typical distances for running routes around Benifato?
A: Running routes in Benifato vary significantly in length due to the mountainous terrain. You can find shorter, challenging loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872617324/sierra-de-aitana-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Aitana Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; at 7 km (4.4 miles), or longer, more demanding runs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10357269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from Benimantell&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 23 km (14.4 miles).

Q: Where can I find parking when planning a run in Benifato?
A: Parking in Benifato is generally available within the village itself, often near the main square or church. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as some popular starting points may have small designated parking areas. Given the rural nature of the area, roadside parking near trail access points is also common.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the running trails in Benifato?
A: Public transport options directly to Benifato are limited, as it is a small mountain village. It's generally recommended to travel by car to access the running trails. Some regional bus services might connect to larger nearby towns like Callosa d'en Sarrià or Altea, from where you would need to arrange further transport to Benifato.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Benifato?
A: Due to the challenging and mountainous nature of the terrain, most running routes around Benifato are not typically classified as family-friendly for younger children or those seeking very easy, flat paths. The routes often involve significant climbs and descents, making them more suitable for experienced runners or families with older, active children accustomed to strenuous hikes.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running routes in Benifato?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most trails in the Benifato area, especially in the natural mountain environment. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog, as natural water sources might be scarce on some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Benifato?
A: The best time for running in Benifato is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for physical activity. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs necessary, while winters can bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though snow is rare at lower elevations.

Q: What kind of weather conditions should I expect when running in Benifato?
A: Benifato experiences a Mediterranean climate with mountain influences. Summers are hot and dry, often exceeding 30°C (86°F). Winters are mild but can be cool, especially at higher altitudes, with occasional rain. Spring and autumn offer moderate temperatures. Always check the local forecast before heading out, as weather in mountainous regions can change quickly.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or natural landmarks along the running trails?
A: Yes, the running routes around Benifato offer numerous opportunities to experience stunning natural beauty. You can find impressive natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3023685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Blanca Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/376724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Arc Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, several viewpoints provide panoramic vistas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Relleu Footbridge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Benifato?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Benifato are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17304499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Forata – Simas de Partagat loop from Benifato&lt;/a&gt;, which explores natural springs and unique rock formations, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6902985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de Forata – Simas de Partagat loop from Serra d'Aitana&lt;/a&gt;, leading through mountainous terrain.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Benifato?
A: The running experience in Benifato is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners frequently praise the challenging mountainous terrain, the stunning views of the Sierra de Aitana, and the opportunity to explore natural features like springs and unique rock formations. The diverse topography provides a dynamic and rewarding environment for those seeking a strenuous workout amidst beautiful scenery.

Q: How many running routes are available in Benifato?
A: There are over 60 running routes available around Benifato on komoot. These routes primarily cater to moderate to advanced ability levels, offering a wide range of options for exploring the region's mountainous landscape.
tips: The path passes through the small pylon of the Nogal fountain and much higher, at a crossroads, is the stepped trough of the popular Font de Forata, the highest in the province.

The Aitana (1,558 m) is the highest mountain in the province of Alicante, it rises ruthlessly from the Guadalest valley creating vertiginous steps in the fractures opened by the faults of the enormous anticline on the north slope. On the terraces the villages are hung, which show off an impeccable white print, and some fountains that have been highly appreciated since ancient times for the quality of their waters also appear.

The path enters the Peña Alta hill (1,505 m) and you can take the path to the left to go over the top or continue halfway up the slope, surrounding the summit, to begin the descent of the mountain along the ridge of Alto de the Tagarina (1,434 m). The path runs along the edge of the walls, with impressive views of the Guadalest valley and the mountains and peaks that surround the Aitana massif. The final section of the descent to the Portet de Tagarina pass is steep and rocky, a good place for slips and sprained ankles.
A nice crest. Make sure you walk along the edge. Great view on both sides.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Font de Partegat, which is easily accessible via an asphalted road from Benifato. There is parking available at the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
faqs.answer: This trail is consistently rated as medium to hard difficulty and is more suited for experienced hikers or trail runners with good fitness and sure-footedness. The varied and often technical terrain, including sections like the 'Passet de la Rabosa' which requires scrambling, makes it unsuitable for beginners or continuous casual jogging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular trail run?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll start on forest tracks and paths through cultivated areas like almond groves. As you ascend, it becomes rockier, transitioning to unpaved forest paths and areas with loose scree. A significant feature is the 'Passet de la Rabosa', a technical section involving climbing over rocks and passing through narrow crevices. Expect rugged, high-altitude sections, canyons, and vertical walls.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points. These include the Font de Forata, the highest spring in Alicante province. You'll also pass near the summit of Aitana (1,558 m), and enjoy views from Peña Alta (1,505 m) and Alto de la Tagarina (1,434 m). The Alt de Tagarina Ridge offers stunning panoramic views of the northern face of the Aitana mountain range, the Guadalest Valley, and even the Mediterranean Sea.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience this trail run?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is generally spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. In summer, there is little shade, making it very hot, so an early start is crucial. In winter, snow and ice can make the route considerably more difficult and dangerous, potentially requiring specialized gear like crampons and an ice axe.

faqs.question: Is the La Serra d'Aitana circular trail run dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Aitana, the technical nature of this specific trail, especially sections like the 'Passet de la Rabosa' which requires scrambling, might be challenging or even unsafe for some dogs. Consider your dog's agility and experience before bringing them on this route.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Sierra de Aitana?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails in the Sierra de Aitana, including this circular route from Font del Partagat. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Are there water sources or shaded areas along the trail?
faqs.answer: There is little shade along much of the route, especially at higher elevations, making sun protection essential. While there are springs like Font de Forata, it's crucial to carry enough water for the entire duration of your run, as natural sources can be unreliable or seasonal.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Port de Tudons - Benifato, Port de Tudons - Cim d'Aitana, and various segments of the Camí de Tagarina and Camí d'Aitana trails.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this trail run?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, sturdy trail running shoes with good grip are essential. You should also bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), appropriate layers for changing weather, a map or GPS device, and snacks. For winter conditions, crampons and an ice axe might be necessary.

faqs.question: Are there any particularly challenging or technical sections on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the most notable technical section is the 'Passet de la Rabosa'. This part involves climbing over rocks and navigating through narrow crevices, often requiring the use of your hands. It demands agility and extreme caution, especially if the rocks are wet or icy.

waypoints.name: Peña Alta (1,505 m) and Alto de la Tagarina (1,434 m)
